region_of_origin: United States (Eggs Benedict origin ~1894, New York); Royale variation adapted in British and Australian café culture; adopted in Singapore specialty cafés from ~2010
food_subgroup: Poached eggs on English muffin with smoked salmon and hollandaise sauce; café brunch dish distinguished from Eggs Benedict (Canadian bacon version)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Eggs Royale specifically. Closest parent concept: poached egg open sandwich dish with smoked fish (FOODON:03315763 egg dish, closest parent). No exact match available as of 2026.
ontology.foodex2_description: Eggs royale — poached eggs smoked salmon hollandaise English muffin; FoodEx2 closest parent match for composite egg-based brunch dish with smoked fish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0EBR used as closest parent FoodEx2 code for composite egg-smoked fish brunch dish; no exact FoodEx2 code for Eggs Royale as of 2026.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for Eggs Royale composite dish. Not a clinical entity requiring SNOMED mapping.

serving.description: 1 plate (~340g): 1 English muffin split + 2 poached eggs (~100g) + smoked salmon (~60g) + hollandaise sauce (~60g) + capers (~5g) + dill
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard café serving is one plate of approximately 340g comprising one split English muffin, two poached eggs, approximately 60g smoked salmon, 60g hollandaise sauce, and 5g capers with dill.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — component-based calculation: English muffin (FDC 174903), smoked salmon (FDC 175168), hollandaise sauce (FDC 171249), poached egg; Singapore café calorie disclosure analysis
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (USDA Agricultural Research Service)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from weighted component calculation using USDA FDC entries for each ingredient (English muffin FDC 174903, smoked salmon FDC 175168, hollandaise sauce FDC 171249, poached egg) combined in proportions matching the standard 340g Singapore café serving. Per-100g values represent the composite dish. Singapore café calorie disclosure data used to cross-validate total energy. USDA FDC is the approved primary source for Western composite dishes not in HPB Singapore database.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of English muffin and composite dish estimation — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~57 derived from English muffin GI ~57 per University of Sydney GI Database (white English muffin, closest match). Hollandaise sauce and smoked salmon contain negligible carbohydrates and do not materially affect GI. Composite dish GI dominated by English muffin component. GL calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (57 × 35.7g) / 100 ≈ 20.3; input record states GL ~18.8 using slightly lower available carb estimate; 18.8 retained as consistent with component analysis. Assessment type: referenced analogous estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium — approximately 2,312mg per 340g serving (116% of WHO 2,000mg daily limit); smoked salmon (~720mg Na) is the dominant contributor, compounded by hollandaise (~280mg), English muffin (~300mg), and capers (~200mg)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should treat this dish as an occasional indulgence only; the sodium load of ~2,312mg per serving exceeds the entire WHO daily sodium limit and is primarily driven by the curing and smoking process used for the salmon, making sodium reduction difficult without ingredient substitution.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic load (~GL 19) from English muffin carbohydrates; high fat content from hollandaise sauce moderately slows glucose absorption; caloric density of ~639 kcal per serving is a significant concern for weight management
type2_diabetes.guidance: For patients with Type 2 Diabetes, the medium GL (~19) and fat-slowed glucose absorption make this preferable to white toast-based breakfasts, but the high caloric density (~639 kcal) and saturated fat from hollandaise sauce (5.8g/100g) warrant portion control and infrequent consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Dual cardiovascular signal: beneficial omega-3 EPA+DHA ~1.6–1.9g per serving from smoked salmon (near daily adequate intake) versus high sodium (~2,312mg), high cholesterol (~165mg/100g, ~561mg per serving), and saturated fat (~5.8g/100g) from hollandaise sauce and eggs
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease patients, the significant omega-3 EPA+DHA content from smoked salmon provides anti-inflammatory and cardioprotective benefit, but this must be weighed against the very high sodium load, elevated cholesterol, and saturated fat from hollandaise sauce; this dish is best consumed occasionally and not as a regular dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High omega-3 EPA+DHA (~1.6–1.9g per serving) from smoked salmon provides significant anti-inflammatory benefit; very high sodium (~2,312mg) is the primary concern
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: For rheumatoid arthritis patients, the near-daily adequate intake of EPA+DHA omega-3 from smoked salmon offers meaningful anti-inflammatory benefit, but the very high sodium content (~2,312mg per serving) and caloric density require careful consideration within an overall anti-inflammatory dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Smoked Salmon Eggs Benedict, also known as Eggs Royale, is a premium café brunch dish consisting of two poached eggs and smoked salmon served on a toasted split English muffin, topped with rich hollandaise sauce, capers, and fresh dill. A standard Singapore café serving weighs approximately 340g and delivers ~639 kcal, 35.7g protein, 40.8g fat, and 35.7g carbohydrate. The dish is nutritionally distinguished from classic Eggs Benedict (which uses Canadian bacon) by its significantly higher omega-3 EPA+DHA content (~1.6–1.9g per serving, near the daily adequate intake) and higher sodium load (~2,312mg per serving, 116% of the WHO 2,000mg daily limit). The dominant sodium source is the smoked salmon, whose curing and smoking process introduces approximately 720mg sodium per serving; hollandaise sauce (~280mg), English muffin (~300mg), and capers (~200mg) compound the total. The glycemic index is medium (~57, driven by the English muffin), with a glycemic load of approximately 19 per serving. Hollandaise sauce contributes significant saturated fat (5.8g/100g composite) and cholesterol (165mg/100g). The dish is rich in vitamin B12 (~2.5mcg/100g), vitamin D (~2.2mcg/100g), selenium (~28mcg/100g), and niacin (~3.8mg/100g) from the salmon and egg components. In Singapore, Eggs Royale is a standard menu item at specialty cafés, hotel brunch services, and premium breakfast venues. The dish originated as an American invention (~1894, New York) with the Royale variation substituting smoked salmon for Canadian bacon, a British café adaptation subsequently adopted in Australian and Singapore café culture from approximately 2010. Clinically, the dish offers meaningful omega-3 and micronutrient benefits but requires caution for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients due to its very high sodium and saturated fat content.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central component entries (English muffin FDC 174903, smoked salmon FDC 175168, hollandaise sauce FDC 171249, poached egg) — all approved primary sources. Component proportions match the standard 340g Singapore café serving. Singapore café calorie disclosure data cross-validates total energy. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database for English muffin as the dominant carbohydrate component. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are supported by approved sources.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for the composite dish (English muffin + smoked salmon + hollandaise + poached egg) is not available as a single composite entry in USDA FoodData Central; individual component fatty acid data exists but reliable weighted composite values for the specific Singapore café portion were not calculable with sufficient precision from available approved sources as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite dish is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero per USDA FDC component data for all ingredients in this dish. This record is DISTINCT from Eggs Benedict (Canadian bacon version, Batch 17 id=856, F05020041): smoked salmon provides substantially different omega-3 EPA+DHA content (~1.6–1.9g per serving) and a different sodium source profile compared to cured pork. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate based on English muffin GI from University of Sydney GI Database; the composite dish GI is dominated by the English muffin as the sole significant carbohydrate source. Halal dietary tag preserved from input; standard smoked salmon and hollandaise ingredients are halal-compatible but consumers should verify specific café sourcing. Vitamin A reported as 110 mcg/100g (retinol activity equivalents from egg yolk and salmon); selenium 28 mcg/100g reflects smoked salmon contribution.
